framework.name: RWA HQLA Internal Limits Matrix
framework.disclaimer: The percentages and triggers are uncalibrated scenario inputs for analytical discussion. They are not regulatory limits, model policy, suitable exposure limits or recommendations for any institution. Replace them with institution-specific evidence and governance decisions.

single_product.rationale: Avoid material exposure to a single wrapper plus issuer combination (e.g. BUIDL alone).
single_product.example: For a bank with EUR 100bn liquid assets, max EUR 5bn in any single tokenised product.

single_issuer.rationale: Avoid management concentration across products from the same issuer (e.g. all BlackRock-managed tokenised products combined).
single_issuer.example: For a bank with EUR 100bn liquid assets, max EUR 10bn across all products from one issuer.

single_chain.rationale: Test concentration and operational dependence on a single blockchain; the relevant perimeter is product- and institution-specific.
single_chain.example: Within the tokenised RWA bucket, max 25% deployed on any single blockchain.

single_custodian.rationale: Test operational dependence on a single custody provider; no provider is endorsed.
single_custodian.example: Within the tokenised RWA bucket, max 33% under any single custody provider.

aggregate.rationale: Illustrative concentration scenario only; the repository does not calibrate an appropriate aggregate exposure.
aggregate.example: For a bank with EUR 100bn liquid assets, max EUR 1-2bn aggregate tokenised RWA exposure.

calibration_guidance.policy: No institution type is assigned a default cap. Calibrate from permitted purpose, liquidity testing, legal opinions, prudential treatment, operational capacity and risk appetite.

exit_triggers.action: Example governance response for discussion; define the actual action before any exposure.

exit_triggers.threshold: NAV vs on-chain price divergence > 1% sustained
exit_triggers.action: Example governance response for discussion; define the actual action before any exposure.

exit_triggers.threshold: any confirmed key compromise or unauthorized access
exit_triggers.action: Example governance response for discussion; define the actual action before any exposure.

exit_triggers.threshold: issuer invokes any extraordinary event or liability cap clause (e.g. bIB01 Article XVII)
exit_triggers.action: Example governance response for discussion; define the actual action before any exposure.

exit_triggers.threshold: aggregate exposure > 2.5% of liquid assets (10% over policy cap)
exit_triggers.action: Example governance response for discussion; define the actual action before any exposure.
Example governance response for discussion; define the actual action before any exposure.

haircut_reduction_on_progression.note: No automatic haircut reduction is inferred from L1/L2 progression. Reassess the specific legal structure, market liquidity and operational eligibility.
